Clinical_Notes: Preferred analgesic and antipyretic during pregnancy when used at the lowest effective dose for the shortest duration.
May be used during pregnancy when clinically indicated especially in women with diabetes under medical supervision.
Low-dose aspirin may be prescribed for specific obstetric indications but routine use requires medical supervision.
ACE inhibitors should not be used during pregnancy because of risk of serious fetal harm especially after the first trimester.
May be considered if clinically indicated when benefits outweigh potential risks.
Statins should be discontinued during pregnancy because they may affect fetal development.
Use only if the expected maternal benefit outweighs potential fetal risk.
Widely used during pregnancy when clinically indicated and generally considered safe.
Use when clinically indicated under medical supervision as human data are limited.
Avoid during pregnancy unless no safer alternative exists because fetal risk cannot be excluded.
NSAID-containing combinations should be avoided during the third trimester because of fetal cardiovascular and renal risks.
Avoid during pregnancy particularly in the third trimester because of NSAID exposure and limited safety data for chlorzoxazone.
Telmisartan is contraindicated during pregnancy because of fetal toxicity associated with renin-angiotensin system blockade.
